evals.name: review-implemented-task-name-shadowing
evals.prompt: A spec-builder sub-agent implemented task 01 (passphrase lock). Review its diff semi-formally before it can be marked done. The diff adds an unlock() in src/auth/lock.ts that calls verify(passphrase, hash).
evals.expected_output: The skill produces a certificate, not an opinion. It states P1 (files/functions touched), P2 (what the task asked — gate the vault on the correct passphrase, from Produces/Steps), P3 (what must not break). It runs function resolution on each call in the changed lines via the 5-step sequence — in particular resolving verify() to either the imported crypto verify or a shadowing global/module definition, flagging NAME SHADOWING if both could match. It writes a 3-5 step execution trace for a concrete input (wrong then correct passphrase), runs a regression trace on at least one downstream caller (or states none is in scope), and checks sufficiency (root cause vs symptom). It ends with exactly VERDICT: [CORRECT|LIKELY_CORRECT|CONCERNS|BUGGY], CONFIDENCE, SUMMARY — derived from the checkpoints, not declared up front. The reviewer is noted as distinct from the implementer.

evals.name: skip-trivial-change
evals.prompt: Run a semi-formal review on this diff: it fixes a typo in README.md and bumps a version string in package.json.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognises a skip condition (docs/formatting/version bump, no execution path crossing a scope boundary) and does NOT build a full certificate. It says the change is trivial and why, proceeds with standard reasoning, and — if invoked inside a spec-builder build — records that the correctness gate was skipped as trivial rather than silently bypassed. No fabricated VERDICT ceremony for a no-op.
